message:
fix(http): drop IO on rejected pool transitions, assert frame length, fix active stream filter

HTTP client (h1/http_client.rs):
- release_connection now checks the bool return from pool.mark_connected
  and pool.release. When the transition is rejected (e.g., releasing a
  Connecting connection or double-releasing), the ClientIo is dropped
  and the pool entry is cleaned up instead of storing orphaned idle IO
  that can never be reclaimed.
- Added tests: release_connection_drops_fresh_io_when_mark_connected_
  is_rejected, release_connection_drops_reused_io_when_release_is_rejected.

H2 frame encoding (h2/frame.rs):
- FrameHeader::write: promote debug_assert! to assert! for the 24-bit
  length limit. Release builds previously would silently truncate the
  length field, causing the header to declare fewer bytes than were
  actually written, violating the H2 framing contract.
- frame_length: replace silent clamping with assert!, preventing
  encoders from constructing headers that disagree with payload size.
- Added test: frame_length panics above MAX_FRAME_SIZE.

H2 stream store (h2/stream.rs):
- active_stream_ids now uses is_active() instead of !is_closed(),
  matching the predicate used by active_count() so the invariant
  active_stream_ids().len() == active_count() always holds. The
  is_active predicate excludes half-closed and reset states that
  !is_closed would include.

HTTP pool (pool.rs):
- Pool::mark_connected and Pool::release now return bool (was void),
  propagating the guarded transition result to callers.
- All test assertions updated to check transition return values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
